sql_findings.query_summary: Check for shared device fingerprints and IP addresses among flagged withdrawals.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified two distinct device fingerprints shared by multiple customers. One fingerprint ('deadbeef...') linked two Egyptian accounts; another ('a1b2c3d4...') linked a Russian and a French account.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyze transaction success rates and payment method variety.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed card testing behavior in one account (3 failures out of 4 attempts with 'card_restricted' errors) and high card error history indicators for all others.

sql_findings.query_summary: Investigate trading duration and frequency.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found that flagged accounts typically perform only 1-2 trades, often lasting less than 60 seconds, which is inconsistent with genuine investment behavior.

web_references.title: Identity Fraud Protection with Device Fingerprinting
web_references.relevance: Describes the use of device fingerprinting to link coordinated fraud accounts.

web_references.title: Real-Time Fraud Monitoring for Enterprise Fintech Stacks
web_references.relevance: Explains card testing and velocity patterns in fintech.

sql_findings.query_summary: Investigation of geographic risk and device fingerprint indicators for recent blocked withdrawals.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified multiple customers (47f31044..., 80320da3..., 851b3a09...) with high geographic risk scores (0.78-0.90) and device anomalies.

sql_findings.query_summary: Checking for shared IP addresses across different customer accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed that IP 41.44.55.66 (Cairo) was used by two distinct customers, suggesting a shared attacker origin. IP 185.199.108.55 (VPN) was used for 5 consecutive attempts on one account.

web_references.title: Impossible Travel: How Hunters Detects Account Takeover
web_references.relevance: Defines 'impossible travel' as a primary detection mechanism for ATO.

web_references.title: Visa Public: Financial Fraud: Account Takeover Guide
web_references.relevance: Visa's guide on spotting and preventing ATO via location and device anomalies.

title: Pattern: financial_behavior
formal_pattern_name: Collusive Credit Card Cashing / Money Laundering Ring

sql_findings.query_summary: Identification of customers with high withdrawal-to-deposit ratios and minimal trading.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified 4 primary accounts (Ahmed Hassan, Fatima Nour, Victor Petrov, Sophie Laurent) with 0-2 trades and withdrawal amounts nearly equal to deposits.

sql_findings.query_summary: Investigation of shared payment methods, devices, and IP addresses.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed that Ahmed Hassan and Fatima Nour share IP 41.44.55.66, device 'deadbeef...', and Visa 4444. Victor Petrov and Sophie Laurent share device 'a1b2c3d4...' and Visa 1111.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analysis of withdrawal recipients and bank accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Discovered that multiple accounts withdraw to the same recipient names (Elena Volkov, Chen Wei) and one specific Egyptian bank account was shared between two users.

web_references.title: Mastering AML Typologies Through Capital Markets - NICE Actimize
web_references.relevance: Describes 'One-Directional Trading' and money laundering typologies where platforms are used as conduits for illicit funds.

sql_findings.query_summary: Device and Customer Overlap Analysis
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified multiple instances where a single device fingerprint was shared by customers in different countries (e.g., France and Russia, Egypt).

sql_findings.query_summary: Transaction Timing and Velocity
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed a rapid 'deposit-to-withdrawal' cycle where funds are moved out of the system within 24 hours of arrival. Found multiple 'card_restricted' errors preceding successful deposits.

sql_findings.query_summary: Trading Activity Audit
sql_findings.result_summary: Determined that target accounts have near-zero economic activity, with PnL and trade volumes being negligible compared to deposit amounts.

sql_findings.query_summary: Payment Method Fingerprinting
sql_findings.result_summary: Discovered that the same Visa card (last four digits) was being used across different accounts that also shared devices, confirming a coordinated ring.

web_references.title: 14 Crypto Scam Types and Forensic Patterns
web_references.relevance: Describes the 'pass-through' behavior and shared infrastructure common in crypto/trading fraud rings.

web_references.title: Dictionary of Fraud Terms: ATO and Application Fraud
web_references.relevance: Defines Account Takeover and Application Fraud which align with the observed carding and shared device patterns.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify customers with high withdrawals and zero trades.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found customer 80320da3... with $18,326.21 in withdrawals and 0 trades within 7 days of registration.

sql_findings.query_summary: Check for shared infrastructure among suspicious accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified IP 41.44.55.66 and device 'deadbeef...' shared by two accounts that both sent money to the same recipient account.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyze withdrawal recipients for the primary suspect.
sql_findings.result_summary: The suspect sent funds to four different names ('Self', 'Elena Volkov', 'John Doe', 'Mohamed Nour') using different IPs for each, a tactic used to avoid detection.

web_references.title: The Role of Credit Cards in Money Laundering - Flagright
web_references.relevance: Describes the 'rapid loading and unloading' of funds as a key money laundering indicator.

web_references.title: Cashout | Intel 471
web_references.relevance: Defines 'Cashout' as the final stage of fraud where illicit proceeds are moved to the actor.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify customers sharing device fingerprints and recipient accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found Ahmed Hassan and Fatima Nour sharing device 'deadbeef...' and recipient 'EG800002000156789012345678901'.

sql_findings.query_summary: Scan for other multi-customer devices active recently.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified a second device 'a1b2c3d4...' shared by Victor Petrov and Sophie Laurent.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyze withdrawal timing across all linked accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: All four accounts (across two devices) requested withdrawals at exactly 12:00:00 UTC on 2026-02-08.

web_references.title: The Modern Fraud Ring — And Why Collaborative SARs Are the Key to Stopping It
web_references.relevance: Describes the shift toward coordinated fraud rings using shared identifiers.

web_references.title: Money Mule Networks & Collusion
web_references.relevance: Explains the use of link analysis to detect mule networks sharing devices and IPs.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify accounts sharing device fingerprints and recipients.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found two accounts (ahmed.hassan@example.com and fatima.nour@example.com) sharing device 'deadbeef...', IP '41.44.55.66', and recipient 'Mohamed Nour'.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyze registration and withdrawal timing.
sql_findings.result_summary: Accounts were registered at the exact same second (2026-02-01 12:00:00) and withdrew at the exact same second (2026-02-08 12:00:00).

sql_findings.query_summary: Search for broader impact by recipient account.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed that only these two accounts have sent funds to the identified recipient IBAN.

web_references.title: Account Takeover Fraud: Detection & Prevention
web_references.relevance: Describes the general mechanics of account takeover and fraud rings targeting financial institutions.

title: Pattern: constellation_analysis
formal_pattern_name: Collusive Cashing-Out Ring with Balance Manipulation

sql_findings.query_summary: Comparison of total deposits vs. total withdrawals for flagged customers.
sql_findings.result_summary: Multiple customers (e.g., 80320da3..., 47f31044...) requested withdrawals exceeding $18,000 despite total deposits under $2,000 and near-zero trading activity.

sql_findings.query_summary: Device fingerprint overlap analysis.
sql_findings.result_summary: Fingerprints like 'deadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeef' and 'a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4' are shared across multiple high-risk accounts, confirming a collusive ring.

web_references.title: Fraud Typologies in Financial Institutions
web_references.relevance: Discusses standard carding and cashing out typologies involving new accounts and rapid withdrawals.

web_references.title: Card Decline Codes and Fraud Detection
web_references.relevance: Explains the significance of card decline/error codes in detecting fraudulent rings.
